summary.intent: Build a fully interactive browser-in-browser feature for the moltis AI assistant, including a UI to view/control browser sessions, sharing sessions between the AI agent and the settings UI, and session history/recording for post-hoc review.
summary.outcome: Core browser viewing and interaction works end-to-end: agent-created sessions appear in the UI, screencasts stream live, clicks/scroll relay to CDP, URL bar syncs with the remote browser, and session persistence with action logging was added. Several critical bugs were found and fixed along the way.

repo: serde's #[serde(default)] only handles *missing* keys, not explicit `null` — LLMs (MiniMax) send null for every optional field, requiring a null-strip pass before deserialization
The WebSocket broadcast layer has an explicit subscription filter (broadcast.rs ~L100) — events not in the client's subscription list are silently dropped with no error
BrowserTool and RealBrowserService originally had separate BrowserManager instances — sharing via Arc<OnceCell> is the correct pattern for shared lazy initialization
CDP Page.startScreencast relay task exits immediately when tx.send() finds zero receivers — the relay must drop frames silently when no subscribers rather than exit
CDP Input.dispatchMouseEvent with 'mouseWheel' type requires BOTH deltaX and deltaY to be present, even when one is 0 — omitting either returns a CDP error
The canvas useEffect([], ...) pattern misses late DOM renders — a ref callback (canvasRefCallback) fires on every DOM attach/detach and is the correct approach for conditionally-rendered canvases
Rapid screencast stop/start cycles (on session switch) overwhelm CDP and kill the Chrome connection — screencasts should remain running in the background, with frame listeners filtering by session_id
db_pool is created significantly after the browser service in server.rs — use Arc<OnceCell> for deferred store injection rather than restructuring startup order
The BrowserService trait is in service-traits crate which does not have sqlx as a dependency — concrete store types must be injected via concrete methods, not trait methods
Screencast metadata offset_top must be added to the y-coordinate for click accuracy — without it clicks appear higher than intended

code.finding: Null-stripping via obj.retain(|_, v| !v.is_null()) must happen before BrowserRequest deserialization to handle LLM null injection
Relay task originally exited on zero receivers — changed to drop frames silently so late subscribers (UI connecting after screencast started) can still receive frames
browser.screencast.frame was missing from the WebSocket subscription list, causing all screencast frames to be silently dropped by the broadcast filter
Subscription filter: events are only forwarded to clients that explicitly subscribed — missing subscription = silent drop, no error surfaced anywhere
CDP mouseWheel dispatch requires both deltaX and deltaY; original code omitted whichever was 0, causing CDP to reject all scroll events
Default viewport was 2560x1440@2x (agent-optimized) — websites rendered for that resolution appeared tiny/misaligned in the viewer; changed to 1440x900@1x
has_session() guard prevents cleanup spam — when Chrome dies and 50 queued events try to cleanup the session, only the first succeeds; subsequent calls return early
validate_url() blocked about:blank, causing silent failure when creating new sessions from the UI — added explicit allowlist for about:blank

workflow: When CDP returns success:false with HTTP 200, callers get no error — must check res.success explicitly; centralizing this in the fetch wrapper prevents silent failures
Integration tests with real CDP (marked #[ignore]) are essential for browser interaction — unit tests can't catch CDP protocol constraints like the deltaX/deltaY requirement
Playwright e2e tests should validate the full stack including WebSocket subscription setup, not just DOM rendering
When a feature needs two async-initialized components to share state, Arc<OnceCell<T>> with a deferred set() call after initialization is the idiomatic Rust pattern
Browser sessions should keep screencasts running in background when switching views — the frame listener can filter by session_id, avoiding the CDP stress from rapid stop/start

friction: YubiKey tap required for every git push — caused multiple push failures and retry loops throughout the session
serde #[serde(default)] vs explicit null ambiguity caused an obscure deserialization panic that required reading through serde internals to understand

The root cause of 'Waiting for first frame' required three separate debugging rounds: relay race condition, then navigate URL validation, then missing WebSocket subscription — each fix revealed the next layer
db_pool created after browser service in server.rs — required adding OnceCell-based deferred injection to avoid restructuring the entire server startup sequence
A replace_all edit accidentally modified an unrelated session_store field in GatewayServices, causing a compile error that was confusing to diagnose
sqlx types unavailable in service-traits crate prevented adding trait methods that take SqlitePool — required keeping a concrete Arc<RealBrowserService> reference alongside the trait object
Rapid session switching during debugging killed Chrome connections due to stop/start cycle — root cause was architectural but manifested as flaky behavior
open_items: WebRTC upgrade (neko-style) for sub-100ms latency and 6-18x bandwidth reduction vs JPEG screencast
Binary WebSocket frames for screencast — eliminates ~33% base64 overhead
Per-agent browser profiles for session isolation (currently single global profile)
Session history UI — backend persistence (SQLite store, migration, API endpoints) was implemented but the UI to show closed sessions with logs/screenshots was not built
Browser connection robustness — Chrome can still die under memory pressure; retry logic added but underlying container stability needs more work
Session recording as video — the feature was requested but only action logging was implemented; JPEG timeline or video export not yet done
Favicon support in URL autocomplete dropdown
